name: Band
description: Temasek Polytechnic Band (TP Band) is a student music ensemble that performs a wide range of repertoire, including classical, contemporary, popular, and cinematic works. Members develop musical skills and ensemble performance through structured rehearsals, sectionals, and opportunities to perform at concerts, festivals, and community events.
training:
Tuesday, 7pm to 9pm
 Friday, 7pm to 9pm (Sectionals)
 Ad-hoc tutelage
achievements: Temasek Student Excellence Awards: Arts Group of the Year (2025)
Tapestry Arts Festival: Metamorphosis - Avec L’Expression XIV: Intarsia (2024)
Singapore International Band Festival - Gold Award (Division 1) (2024)

name: Chinese Orchestra
description: Temasek Polytechnic Chinese Orchestra (TPCO) is a student orchestra that performs both traditional and contemporary Chinese music. Members develop musical technique, ensemble skills, and cultural expression through rehearsals, performances, collaborations, and community outreach events.

name: Chorale
description: Temasek Polytechnic Chorale (TP Chorale) is a student choir that performs a diverse range of choral repertoire, including classical, contemporary, and musical theatre works. Members develop vocal technique, musicality, and ensemble skills through structured rehearsals, performances, festivals, and competitions.
achievements: Tapestry: Flux - EVOS IX: Go the Distance (2025)
Project Red Dot - SG60 Celebrations @ Gardens by the Bay (2025)
Per Musicam Ad Astra 11th International Copernicus Choir Festival & Competition: Gold Diploma ("Mixed Choirs” & “Folklore” Categories, 2025)
Golden Voices of Montserrat: Gold Diploma & Silver Diploma (“Academic Singing” & “Folklore" Categories, 2016)

name: Dance Ensemble (Modern, Hip Hop & BBoys)
description: Temasek Polytechnic Dance Ensemble (TPDE) is a student dance group specialising in Hip Hop, Modern Dance, and Breakin'. Members develop their technique, creativity, and stage presence through structured training, performances, and competitions, with opportunities to showcase their talents at institutional, national, and public events.
training:
Modern: Tuesdays, 6pm to 9pm
Hip Hop: Wednesdays, 6pm to 9pm
Breakin’: Thursdays, 6pm to 9pm
achievements: World Supremacy Battlegrounds 2024 (First runner-up for Tertiary Category)

name: Digital Media and Production Crew
description: Digital Media & Production Crew (DMPC) is a student production team that specialises in stage management, lighting, audio, photography, videography, and graphic design. Members gain hands-on experience through training and live event productions, developing technical skills and teamwork while supporting a wide range of campus events and performances.
training:
Live Sound: Mondays, 7pm to 9pm
Production Lighting: Wednesdays, 7pm to 9pm
Photography & Videography: Thursdays, 7pm to 9pm
Graphics: Alternate Fridays, 7pm to 9pm
advisor: Ms Jasmine Leong & Mr Nigel Tan

name: Dikir Barat
description: Temasek Polytechnic Dikir Barat is a student Malay performing arts group that combines vocals, percussion, and lyrical storytelling in dynamic team-based performances. Members develop their musical and performance skills while participating in cultural events, productions, and competitions that celebrate Malay heritage and creativity.
achievements: Tapestry Arts Festival: Jom Berdikir with TPDK (2024-2025)
Piala Suara Serumpun 2025 (Women’s): Best Awok-Awok, Best Paluan, Best Costume, Nominated for Best Senikata
Piala Suara Serumpun 2025 (Men’s):  Nominated for Best Tukang Karut
Piala Suara Serumpun 2024 (Women’s): Third Placing, Best Senikata, Nominated for Best Juara and Awok-Awok
Piala Suara Serumpun 2024 (Men’s): Third Placing, Nominated for Best Juara, Best Tukang Karut and Best Awok-Awok
Piala Suara Serumpun 2023 (Women’s): Nominated for Best Tukang Karut and Best Awok-Awok

name: Fusion Percussion
description: Temasek Polytechnic Fusion Percussion (TPFP) is a student percussion ensemble that combines rhythm, movement, and musical creativity to deliver energetic and engaging performances. Members develop their timing, precision, teamwork, and stage presence through structured training and opportunities to perform at campus and public events.

name: Indian Dance
description: Temasek Polytechnic Indian Dance Group (TPIDG) is a student dance group that showcases a variety of Indian dance styles, including Bharatanatyam, folk, and Bollywood dance. Members develop technique, musicality, and stage presence through training, performances, festivals, and cultural events that celebrate Indian heritage and artistic expression.
achievements: Tapestry Arts Festival: FLUX - Echoes of Tradition (2025)
Performed at "Largest Mass Performance of Indian Traditional Folk Arts in Singapore" (13 July 2024)

name: Malay Dance
description: Temasek Polytechnic Malay Dance Group (Seni Nak Tari / SNT) is a student dance ensemble that showcases traditional and contemporary Malay dance through expressive performances and cultural storytelling. Members develop technique, artistry, and stage presence through structured training, performances, festivals, and community showcases that celebrate Malay heritage and the performing arts.
achievements: 35th Anniversary Concert: Meraki – Jati Si Anak Tari (2025)
Tapestry Arts Festival: FLUX - Jom Jom SNT (2025)

name: Music Vox
description: Temasek Polytechnic Music Vox (MV) is a student music collective that brings together singers, musicians, songwriters, and performers to create and perform pop, rock, indie, and original music. Members develop their musical and creative skills through live performances, songwriting, recording projects, and industry collaborations that provide real-world production experience.
training:
Sectional Training (Once fortnightly or monthly), 7pm to 9pm
Rehearsal Jamming (subject to upcoming events)
achievements: Music Vox Annual Concert: Echo @ *SCAPE Ground Theatre (2026)
Backstage Pass with Music & Drama Company on Power98 Love Songs (2022)

name: Salvo Drums
description: Temasek Polytechnic Salvo Drums is a student percussion ensemble specialising in the 24-Season Drums, combining rhythmic drumming, synchronised movement, and visual storytelling. Members develop discipline, teamwork, precision, and performance skills through structured training, cultural showcases, and community performances.
achievements: Gima Band x Salvo Drums Music Video (2026)
Salvo Drums BBT Series: Stories Beneath the Beats (2026)

name: Teatro
description: Temasek Polytechnic Teatro is a student theatre ensemble that focuses on acting, devised theatre, and collaborative storytelling. Members gain experience in both performance and production roles, including scriptwriting, directing, stage management, lighting, sound, and costume design, while participating in productions, showcases, and industry-linked projects.
achievements: Tapestry Arts Festival - Shredded Notes: Melody (with Music Vox) (2024 & 2025)
Singapore Odyssea at National Museum of Singapore (2025)
Black Box Theatre Series: Billie and the Big Book (2025)

name: TP Emcees
description: Temasek Polytechnic Emcees is a student hosting and communications group that develops confident presenters, hosts, and content creators. Members are trained in public speaking, event hosting, audience engagement, and communication skills while supporting campus events and creating content that connects and engages the TP community.

name: 10 Pin Bowling
description: Temasek Polytechnic Bowling welcomes bowlers of all skill levels to develop their technique and compete in a supportive team environment. Members have opportunities to represent TP in local competitions, including the POL-ITE and IVP Games, while building teamwork and sportsmanship.
achievements: POL-ITE Games 2025: Gold (Men's Team) & Silver (Women's Team)

name: Aikido
description: Temasek Polytechnic Aikido introduces members to the Japanese martial art of Aikido, focusing on self-defence techniques that use an opponent's momentum through throws and joint locks. Members develop discipline, coordination, confidence, and self-defence skills through structured training in a supportive environment.

name: Archery
description: Temasek Polytechnic Archery welcomes members to develop their accuracy, focus, and shooting technique through structured training. Members have opportunities to represent TP in local competitions, including the POL-ITE Archery Games and other external tournaments.

name: Badminton
description: Temasek Polytechnic Badminton welcomes players of all skill levels to develop their technique, agility, and competitive skills through regular training. Members have opportunities to represent TP in local competitions, including the POL-ITE and IVP Badminton Games.

name: Basketball
description: Temasek Polytechnic Basketball welcomes players to develop their technical skills, teamwork, and game awareness through regular training and competitive play. Members have opportunities to represent TP in local competitions, including the POL-ITE and IVP Basketball Games.
training:
Monday & Wednesday, 7pm (Women's Team)
Tuesday & Thursday, 7pm (Men's Team)

name: Dragon Boat
description: Temasek Polytechnic Dragon Boat welcomes members to develop strength, endurance, teamwork, and paddling technique through regular training. Members have opportunities to represent TP in local competitions, including the POL-ITE Dragon Boat Race and other external races.

name: Field Hockey
description: Temasek Polytechnic Hockey welcomes players to develop their technical skills, teamwork, and game strategy through structured training and competitive play. Members have opportunities to represent TP in local competitions, including the POL-ITE Hockey Games.
training:
Monday & Wednesday, 7pm (Women’s Team)
Tuesday & Thursday, 7pm (Men’s Team)
achievements: POL-ITE Games 2025: Gold (Men's Team) & Silver (Women's Team)

name: Floorball
description: Temasek Polytechnic Floorball welcomes players to develop their stick skills, speed, teamwork, and game strategy through regular training and competitive play. Members have opportunities to represent TP in local competitions, including the POL-ITE and IVP Floorball Games.

name: Handball
description: Temasek Polytechnic Handball welcomes players to develop their passing, shooting, teamwork, and game strategy through regular training and competitive play. Members have opportunities to represent TP in external handball competitions.

name: Inline Skating
description: Temasek Polytechnic Inline Skating welcomes skaters of all skill levels to develop their skating techniques, balance, agility, and control through structured training. Members improve their fitness and confidence while learning a variety of skating skills and manoeuvres.

name: Judo
description: Temasek Polytechnic Judo welcomes members to learn the Japanese martial art of Judo, developing throwing techniques, grappling skills, discipline, and physical fitness through structured training. Members have opportunities to represent TP in external competitions while building confidence, resilience, and sportsmanship.

name: Karate
description: Temasek Polytechnic Karate welcomes members to learn the Japanese martial art of Karate, developing striking techniques, discipline, confidence, and physical fitness through structured training. Members have opportunities to represent TP in external competitions while building character, resilience, and sportsmanship.

name: Kayak Racing
description: Temasek Polytechnic Kayak Racing welcomes members to develop paddling technique, endurance, and water sports skills through structured training. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE Canoe Sprints and external kayaking competitions while building teamwork, fitness, and resilience.
training:
Tuesday & Wednesday, 6.30pm (TP Sports Complex)
Saturday, 8.30am (Bedok Reservoir)
achievements: POL-ITE Games 2025: Gold (Men’s Team) & Bronze (Women’s Team)

name: Mind Sports
description: Temasek Polytechnic Mind Sports welcomes students who enjoy strategy board games such as Chess and Xiangqi. Members develop critical thinking, strategic planning, and problem-solving skills through regular practice and have opportunities to represent TP in external competitions.

name: Netball
description: Temasek Polytechnic Netball welcomes players to develop their passing, shooting, teamwork, and game strategy through structured training and competitive play. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE and IVP Netball Games.

name: Rugby
description: Temasek Polytechnic Rugby welcomes players to develop their strength, teamwork, and tactical skills through structured training and competitive matches. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE Rugby Games while building resilience, discipline, and sportsmanship.

name: Silat
description: Temasek Polytechnic Silat welcomes members to learn the traditional Southeast Asian martial art of Silat, developing striking techniques, discipline, agility, and self-defence skills through structured training. Members have opportunities to represent TP in external competitions, including the IVP Silat competition in both Seni and Tanding categories.

name: Soccer
description: Temasek Polytechnic Soccer welcomes players to develop their technical skills, teamwork, and tactical awareness through structured training and competitive matches. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE Soccer Games and the SR Nathan Challenge Trophy while building fitness, discipline, and sportsmanship.
training:
Monday & Thursday, 7pm (Men's Team)
Tuesday & Wednesday, 7pm (Women's Team)
achievements: POL-ITE Games 2025: Gold (Men's Team) & Silver (Women's Team)

name: Sport Climbing
description: Temasek Polytechnic Sport Climbing welcomes climbers to develop their strength, technique, agility, and problem-solving skills through structured bouldering training. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE Sport Climbing (Bouldering) Championship while building confidence, resilience, and perseverance.

name: Swimming
description: Temasek Polytechnic Swimming welcomes swimmers to develop their technique, speed, endurance, and competitive skills through structured training. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE and IVP Swimming Championships while improving fitness, discipline, and sportsmanship.
achievements: POL-ITE Games 2025: Gold (Men’s Team) & Silver (Women’s Team)
IVP Games (AY2025/26): Silver (Men's Team) & Bronze (Team Overall)

name: Table Tennis
description: Temasek Polytechnic Table Tennis welcomes players to develop their technique, reflexes, and tactical skills through structured training and competitive play. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE and IVP Table Tennis Games while building discipline, precision, and sportsmanship.
achievements: POL-ITE Games 2025: Silver (Men’s Team) & Silver (Women's Team)

name: Taekwondo
description: Temasek Polytechnic Taekwondo welcomes members to learn the Korean martial art of Taekwondo, developing kicking techniques, discipline, confidence, and physical fitness through structured training. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE Taekwondo Games and external competitions in both poomsae and kyorugi.

name: Tchoukball
description: Temasek Polytechnic Tchoukball welcomes players to develop their throwing accuracy, teamwork, and tactical skills through structured training and competitive play. Members build agility, coordination, and sportsmanship while enjoying a fast-paced, non-contact team sport.
training:
Monday & Friday, 7pm (Women's Team)
Tuesday & Friday, 7pm (Men's Team)

name: Tennis
description: Temasek Polytechnic Tennis welcomes players to develop their racquet skills, footwork, and tactical awareness through structured training and competitive play. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE Tennis Games while building fitness, discipline, and sportsmanship.
achievements: POL-ITE Games 2025: Gold (Men's Team) & Bronze (Women's Team)

name: Touch Football
description: Temasek Polytechnic Touch Football is a fast-paced, non-contact team sport that welcomes players to develop their passing, agility, teamwork, and tactical skills through structured training. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE and IVP Touch Football Games while building fitness, confidence, and sportsmanship.

name: Track & Field
description: Temasek Polytechnic Track & Field welcomes athletes to develop speed, strength, endurance, and technical skills across running, jumping, and throwing events. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE and IVP Track & Field Championships while building discipline, fitness, and competitive spirit.

name: Ultimate Frisbee
description: Temasek Polytechnic Ultimate Frisbee welcomes players to develop their throwing, catching, agility, and teamwork skills through structured training and competitive play. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE and IVP Ultimate Frisbee Games while building fitness, strategy, and sportsmanship.

name: Volleyball
description: Temasek Polytechnic Volleyball welcomes players to develop their skills in passing, setting, spiking, and teamwork through structured training and competitive play. Members have opportunities to represent TP in the POL-ITE and IVP Volleyball Games while building agility, coordination, and sportsmanship.
achievements: POL-ITE Games 2025: Gold (Men’s Team), Silver (Women’s Team)

name: Adventure Club
description: Temasek Polytechnic Adventure Club (TPAC) welcomes students of all experience levels to explore the outdoors through activities such as trekking, kayaking, caving, mountaineering, river scrambling, camps, and overseas expeditions. Members build confidence, resilience, leadership, teamwork, and a spirit of adventure while forming lasting friendships through shared outdoor experiences.

name: Applied Science Studies Club
description: Applied Science Studies Club focuses on the personal growth and holistic development of Applied Science students through leadership opportunities, student engagement activities, community service, and school-wide events. Members build teamwork, leadership, and meaningful connections while contributing to campus life and social causes.
keywords: Temasek Polytechnic Applied Science Studies Club
advisor: Ms Seetoh Pei Jia, Dr Lee Koon Guan & Mr Alden Toh

name: Business Studies Club
description: Business Studies Club (BSC) empowers Business students through leadership, event planning, community service, and student engagement activities. Members organise school-wide programmes, leadership camps, arts and sports events, while developing teamwork, communication, and leadership skills.
advisor: Ms Amelia Nadia Ghazali M & Mr Simon Lee

name: Community Service Club
description:
Temasek Polytechnic Community Service Club (TPCSC) brings together students with a passion for giving back through meaningful volunteering and community service. Members support causes such as eldercare, children and family services, special needs, and other outreach projects while developing compassion, leadership, teamwork, and a strong spirit of service.
Email: csc@student.tp.edu.sg

name: Design Studies Club
description: Design Studies Club (DSC) empowers Design students through leadership, event planning, and student engagement activities. Members organise school-wide programmes such as Freshmen Orientation Camp, Director's Cup, and Appreciation Day while developing leadership, teamwork, communication, and project management skills.
advisor: Mr Joshua Lim & Mr Julian Siau

name: Engineering Studies Club
description: Engineering Studies Club (ESC) is the student leadership body for the School of Engineering, organising school-wide events, leadership programmes, student welfare initiatives, and community service activities. Members develop leadership, teamwork, event management, and communication skills while enriching campus life and supporting the Engineering student community.
advisor: Mr Adrian Chia & Mr Andrew Tham (Co-Advisor)

name: Humanities & Social Sciences Studies Club
description: HSS Studies Club enriches the student experience by providing leadership, wellness, community service, and personal development opportunities for students in the School of Humanities & Social Sciences. Through workshops, camps, career talks, and community projects, members develop empathy, leadership, teamwork, and meaningful connections while making a positive impact on the community.
keywords: school of humanities and social sciences
advisor: Ms Lim Chinn Lynette  & Mr Emil Cheong

name: Informatics & IT Studies Club
description: Informatics & IT Studies Club (IITSC) represents students from the School of Informatics & IT by organising leadership, student engagement, volunteerism, and community initiatives. Members develop leadership, teamwork, confidence, and professional skills while contributing to campus life and using technology to make a positive impact.
keywords: Temasek Polytechnic Informatics & IT Studies Club

name: Sports Club
description: Temasek Polytechnic Sports Club (TPSC) promotes an active and inclusive campus by organising sports, wellness, fundraising, and community engagement activities. Members develop leadership, teamwork, and event management skills while encouraging healthy lifestyles, inclusivity, and meaningful connections across the TP community.

name: Students' Union
description: Temasek Polytechnic Students' Union (TPSU) is the largest student organisation in TP, empowering students to lead initiatives that create a vibrant and caring campus. Members develop leadership, event management, teamwork, and communication skills while organising meaningful activities that enrich student life.

name: Business Ambassadors
description: Business Ambassadors (BA) is a student leadership group representing the TP School of Business at official events and outreach programmes. Members develop professional communication, hosting, public speaking, and leadership skills while serving as ambassadors for TP at internal and external functions.

name: Bizfluencers
description: Bizfluencers is a student content creation team representing the TP School of Business through social media, photography, videography, and storytelling. Members develop creative, communication, and digital media skills while showcasing student life and producing engaging content for the Business community.
advisor: Ms Long Li Yann, Ms Selene Goh, Ms Teo Xuan Xuan & Mr Elliott Danker

name: Creative Tech Interest Group
description: Creative Tech Interest Group is a multidisciplinary community that explores the use of Generative AI in design and creative innovation. Bringing together students from various design disciplines, members collaborate on creative projects, experiment with emerging technologies, and develop future-ready skills by integrating AI into design practices while fostering creativity, innovation, and cross-disciplinary collaboration.

name: Design Student Ambassadors
description: Design Student Ambassadors are student leaders who represent the TP School of Design at official events, exhibitions, and outreach programmes. Members develop leadership, communication, public speaking, and event management skills while serving as the face of the Design community by welcoming visitors, conducting school tours, and supporting key school events.

name: ECG Ambassadors
description: ECG Ambassadors Interest Group is a student leadership community that supports Temasek Polytechnic students in exploring education and career pathways. Members represent TP Career Services, promote career and education guidance resources, participate in campus outreach, and develop leadership, communication, peer advising, and career readiness skills while helping fellow students make informed decisions about their future.
advisor: Ms Pan Miaohua (Staff Advisor) & Ms Jo-anne Loh (Co-Advisor)

name: Engineering Makers Club
description: Engineering Makers Club (EMC) is a hands-on maker community that inspires creativity and innovation through engineering projects, workshops, and outreach activities. Members develop practical skills in digital fabrication, programming, prototyping, and engineering design while collaborating on student-led projects, mentoring others, and promoting engineering to the wider community.

name: Engineering Peer Mentors
description: Engineering Peer Mentors (EPM) is a student leadership and service group dedicated to supporting academic success and community engagement. Members organise peer tutoring through the Learning Support Programme, mentor fellow students, and lead community service initiatives while developing leadership, communication, teaching, and volunteerism skills.
advisor: Ms Janessa Thng & Dr Rudy Sabarudin

name: Engineering Student Ambassadors
description: Engineering Student Ambassadors are student leaders who represent the TP School of Engineering at outreach programmes, community events, and official school activities. Members develop leadership, communication, event management, and stakeholder engagement skills while promoting the School of Engineering, inspiring prospective students, and serving as role models within the TP community.
advisor: Mr Andrew Tham  & Ms Wu Chenzhou (Co-Advisor)

name: Fitness Through Science Interest Group
description: Fitness Through Science Interest Group is a student-led wellness community that promotes active and healthy living through science-based fitness initiatives. Members organise health and fitness programmes, inspire peers to adopt healthier lifestyles, and develop leadership, teamwork, and event management skills while championing both physical and mental well-being within the TP community.
keywords: TP Fitness Through Science Interest Group
advisor: Mr Lester Lim, Mr Glendon Teo

name: Glocal Ambassadors
description: Glocal Ambassadors is a student interest group that promotes global citizenship, cultural exchange, and community engagement by connecting international perspectives with local experiences. Members participate in exchanges, cultural immersion programmes, dialogues, and outreach activities while developing leadership, intercultural communication, global awareness, sustainability, and event planning skills.
advisor: Mr Leslie Chan , Ms Lim Lin Da

name: Global Ambassadors (Engineering)
description: Global Ambassadors (Engineering) is a student leadership group that promotes international engagement, cultural exchange, and global learning within the TP School of Engineering. Members host international visitors, support overseas and exchange programmes, organise cross-cultural activities, and develop leadership, communication, hospitality, and global citizenship skills while representing Singapore and Temasek Polytechnic.
training:
Once a month (with Advisor)
Main Committee meetings (vary depending on frequency of events)
advisor: Mr Chua Chiang Heng (Advisor)  & Mr Andrew Tham (Co-advisor)

name: Horticulture & Botany Society
description: Horticulture & Botany Society (HnBSoc) is a student interest group that promotes a passion for plants, gardening, and nature through hands-on workshops, community gardening, and outreach programmes. Members develop practical horticulture skills, participate in environmental initiatives, and engage with the community while fostering sustainability, teamwork, and appreciation for the natural world.
advisor: Dr Kadamb Patel, Mr Chan Kit Lun, Ms Marie Tan & Ms Tan Kay Yee

name: IES-TP Student Chapter
description: The IES-TP Student Chapter is the Temasek Polytechnic student chapter of the Institution of Engineers, Singapore (IES). It provides engineering students with opportunities to connect with industry professionals, participate in workshops, conferences, industry visits, and community service projects, while developing technical knowledge, leadership, networking, and professional engineering skills.
keywords: Institution of Engineers Singapore Student Chapter
advisor: Mr Steven Lee & Mdm Hu Fang (Co-Advisor)

name: Japanese Cultural Group
description: Japanese Cultural Group (JCG) is a student interest group that celebrates Japanese culture through interactive activities, cultural events, exchanges, and immersion programmes. Members explore Japanese traditions, language, arts, entertainment, and cuisine while developing leadership, event management, communication, and cross-cultural skills through local and international experiences.
achievements: Inbound exchange with Fukuoka Institute of Technology (Fukuoka Prefecture) and Fukuyama Municipal Fukuyama High School (Hiroshima Prefecture) (2026)
E-Xchange with staff and students from Kanazawa Commercial High School in Ishikawa Prefecture (KINSHO) (2025-2026)
Event of the Year (EOY) J-Culture Festival (2025)
Overseas Study Trip to Kanazawa, Japan (2024)
advisor: Mr Leslie Chan, Ms Lee Siang Jin & Mr Joachim Lai

name: LEAD Ambassadors
description: LEAD Ambassadors is a student leadership community that empowers members of the Temasek LEADership Programme (Temasek LEAD) to lead, serve, and make meaningful contributions to the LEAD community. Members organise impactful programmes, collaborate with LEAD alumni, and develop leadership, project management, communication, and community engagement skills through planning and facilitating leadership initiatives.

name: Visual Arts & Writing Club
description: The Writing Interest Group is a creative community for students passionate about writing, storytelling, and visual expression. Members explore diverse writing styles, develop creative portfolios, and express ideas on contemporary issues through written and visual works while building communication, creativity, critical thinking, and collaborative skills.

name: Peer Supporters-in-Action
description: Peer Supporters-in-Action (PSiA) is a student-led community that promotes mental well-being, peer support, and a caring campus culture. Members receive peer support training, organise mental health awareness initiatives, and collaborate with the TP community to reduce stigma while developing leadership, empathy, communication, and community engagement skills.
keywords: You've Got a Friend in Me Roadshow

name: SpaceHaus
description: SpaceHaus is an innovation-focused student community that brings together curious minds to explore, create, and collaborate on hands-on projects. Members develop creativity, problem-solving, teamwork, and innovation skills while participating in industry visits, overseas exchanges, and cross-disciplinary experiences that prepare them for future challenges.

name: Sustainability Interest Group
description: Sustainability Interest Group (SUSTIG) is a student-led community that promotes climate action, environmental awareness, and sustainable living through meaningful projects and initiatives. Members participate in green activities, community outreach, overseas learning experiences, and sustainability workshops while developing leadership, teamwork, environmental knowledge, and problem-solving skills to create positive impact.
advisor: Ms Zhang Yu  & Mr Kelvin Lim

name: TP Women in Science and Technology
description: Temasek Polytechnic Women in Science and Technology (TWIST) is a student community that empowers and inspires students to explore STEM pathways through experiential learning, industry exposure, and skill development opportunities. Members participate in symposiums, workplace visits, workshops, and innovation initiatives while building confidence, leadership, and professional skills for future STEM careers.
keywords: Temasek Polytechnic Women in Science and Technology
TP School of Science and Technology
advisor: Dr Rufaihah Abdul Jalil & Ms Louisa Chew

name: SENvocates
description: SENvocates is a student interest group at Temasek Polytechnic that promotes inclusion and advocates for students with disabilities and Special Educational Needs (SEN). Members organise awareness campaigns, collaborate with external partners, and participate in inclusive initiatives while developing empathy, leadership, communication, and community engagement skills to build a more supportive campus environment.

name: TP Toastmasters
description:
TP Toastmasters Club (TPTM) is a student communication and leadership community that helps members build confidence, improve public speaking, and strengthen interpersonal skills in a supportive environment. Through regular chapter meetings, members practise prepared speeches, impromptu speaking, and evaluation skills while developing communication, leadership, and self-expression abilities.
LinkedIn: Temasek Polytechnic Toastmasters
achievements: Organised and hosted the TP Public Speaking Competition for all TP students (2023, 2024)
Champion in the Area S4 International Speech Competition (2025)
Champion in the Area S4 Table Topics Competition (2024, 2025)
Runner-Up in the Area S4 International Speech Competition (2025)
Runner-Up in the Area S4 Table Topics Competition (2025)
Runner-Up in the Area S4 Speech Evaluation Competition (2025)

name: TP Ambassadors
description: TP Ambassadors is an official student representative group that promotes Temasek Polytechnic’s brand through public engagement and digital content creation. Members act as student influencers who showcase campus life, create social media content, and develop media production and communication skills while representing TP as a vibrant and inclusive institution.

name: TP Entrepreneurship Catalyst
description: TP Entrepreneurship Catalyst (TPEC) is a student entrepreneurship community that develops entrepreneurial mindsets through hands-on business experiences, workshops, and competitions. Members gain exposure to startup creation, social entrepreneurship, and industry learning while building skills in innovation, leadership, teamwork, and business development.
training:
Bi-weekly online / offline meetings
Regular monthly events for members and sub-committees
advisor: Mr Ng Shin Leong, Ms Chen Yireng

name: TP-CD Lionhearters
description: Civil Defence Lionhearters Club is a youth volunteer group under the Singapore Civil Defence Force that promotes emergency preparedness, first aid skills, and community safety. Members participate in training, competitions, and outreach activities such as fire station visits, first aid certification, and campus event support while developing leadership, teamwork, and life-saving skills.
keywords: Singapore Global Firefighters and Paramedics Challenge

name: TP Debate Club
description: TP Debate Club is a competitive speaking and critical thinking community that trains students in the art of debate, logic, and persuasion. Members engage in parliamentary-style debates, explore global issues and current affairs, and participate in inter-polytechnic and external competitions while developing communication, analytical thinking, and leadership skills.
achievements: Our past Presidents, Priscilla Chang and Mark Chow, were awarded the prestigious TP CCA Scholarship for their outstanding leadership and contributions to the CCA.

name: TP-Institute of Public Relations of Singapore Student Chapter
description: TP–Institute of Public Relations of Singapore Student Chapter (TP–IPRS SC) is an industry-linked student interest group that promotes awareness and understanding of public relations among TP students. Members develop core PR skills through mentorship programmes, industry talks, and experiential learning opportunities while gaining exposure to communication, branding, and media practices in the PR industry.
keywords: TP Institute of Public Relations of Singapore Student Chapter
Institute of Public Relations of Singapore
achievements: Our past three Presidents of the interest group won the PR Student of the Year at the PRISM Awards, a well-recognised accolade in the PR industry. We have also organised many industry events with leading PR practitioners sharing their thoughts and opinions about the industry.
advisor: Ms Lily Lim & Ms Eunice Chua

name: TP International Students Group
description: TP International Students Group (TPISG) is a student community that supports international students in Temasek Polytechnic by providing a safe, inclusive, and welcoming environment. Members engage in cross-cultural activities, local and international programmes, and community events that promote cultural exchange, integration, and mutual understanding among students from diverse backgrounds.
advisor: Ms Aidli Mosbit (Integration) &  Ms Mindy Nguyen (Welfare)

name: TP Model United Nations
description: TP Model United Nations (TPMUN) is a student interest group that simulates international diplomatic conferences where members debate global issues such as human rights, economics, and environmental challenges. Members develop skills in public speaking, research, critical thinking, and diplomacy through structured training and participation in Model United Nations conferences locally and regionally.
advisor: Ms Charmaine Koh & Mr Allen Roche

name: TP Astronomy
description: Temasek Polytechnic Astronomy is a student interest group that nurtures a passion for astronomy through stargazing sessions, educational activities, and collaborative events. Members explore the universe, learn foundational astronomy concepts, and connect with like-minded peers while developing teamwork, communication, and event organisation skills in a relaxed and engaging environment.
advisor: Mr Moses Jeyandran Danial & Ms Gayatri Bhat

name: PFP Ambassadors
description:
PFP Ambassadors are student leaders who represent the Polytechnic Foundation Programme (PFP) and support both new and prospective students in their transition to polytechnic life. They share personal experiences, assist in school events, and help foster a welcoming, supportive, and connected PFP community.
TikTok: @Pfptp
advisor: Mr Amos Yang, Ms Lynette Lim, Ms Joey Chua, Ms Yew Sok Yee, Ms Joey Neo & Ms Joyce Woon
